role: Lead & Senior Machine Learning Scientist - Intelligent Medical Reporting
bullets: **Designed, developed, and scaled the end-to-end AI service, Report Assistant, from scratch** for Synthesis Health's radiology reporting platform, supporting **30,000+ monthly reports across 160+ facilities**.
Architected agentic LLM workflows for medical report validation, correction, contextual conclusions, and structured output generation.
Restructured the full Report Assistant architecture, reducing inference time from **6 seconds to 2 seconds** for live clinical environments.
Owned full lifecycle delivery across research, prototyping, deployment, and monitoring for **7 major production releases**.

Led and managed a team of **4 ML scientists** to develop and release specialized radiology reporting agents for Breast, Thyroid, and Lung workflows aligned with BI-RADS, TI-RADS, and Lung-RADS standards.
Conceived, architected, and independently built the **Report Preference platform**, enabling per-radiologist and per-clinic AI customization and accelerating feature rollout and retention.
Built AI-native control systems allowing direct user-level manipulation of model behavior from the frontend, eliminating engineering bottlenecks and enabling rapid experimentation.
Created production monitoring and debugging tooling for live agents, enabling proactive issue detection and faster resolution.
metrics: 7 major production releases shipped
Processing 30,000+ reports per month
Deployed across 160+ healthcare facilities
Reduced inference time from 6 seconds to 2 seconds
Managed a team of 4 ML scientists
Built platform for user-level AI customization (Report Preference)
Owned full lifecycle of Report Assistant (research, prototyping, deployment, testing, monitoring)

bullets: **Founded, built, and scaled a multimodal generative AI video platform** delivering lip-sync, voice cloning, audio translation, and word-level video/audio replacements; adopted by **2,800+ B2C users** and enterprise clients, with **150,000+ AI videos generated**.
Architected diffusion-based video generation pipelines with audio-driven synchronization using PyTorch, enabling high-quality, controllable multimodal outputs under GPU-constrained environments.

**Implemented a distributed GPU inference system** using AWS EC2, Kubernetes autoscaling, and Ray; designed a custom GPU job scheduler that dynamically provisions compute and parallelizes workloads, reducing idle GPU costs while sustaining traffic spikes.
**Maintained the full production stack** including frontend, backend, APIs, database, billing, storage, and infrastructure across B2C and enterprise API services.
**Developed enterprise API services**, extending platform capabilities to production-grade B2B use cases.
**Led a team of 4 graduate engineers and researchers** while maintaining full ownership across ML systems, infrastructure, cost management, and product roadmap.
metrics: 2,800+ B2C users
150,000+ videos generated
Enterprise clients
API service
Integration with other platforms
Word level video/audio replacements
Audio cloning and translation
Video generation with lip-sync
Audio Transcriptions
User Interface

bullets: **Led research and development of multi-modal and temporal medical image registration systems** (CT Lung, MRI/CT Head, X-ray Chest, CT/X-ray Spine) using deformable and rigid techniques, enabling longitudinal scan alignment and improving radiologist comparison workflows.
**Designed, trained, and deployed multiple 2D/3D medical image segmentation models** across imaging modalities using deep learning architectures.
**Developed abnormality detection models** for multiple disease conditions using large-scale heterogeneous imaging datasets.
Built ML models for imaging plane detection and MRI sequence classification, improving dataset standardization, preprocessing automation, and downstream pipeline reliability.
Helped operationalize scalable MLOps infrastructure using Docker, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines, accelerating model deployment, reproducibility, and research-to-production iteration cycles.
Contributed to developing asynchronous distributed ML systems using Google Pub/Sub for inter-service communication and orchestration.
Contributed to large-scale medical imaging data processing and training workflows, handling heterogeneous datasets with varying modalities, annotation quality, formats, and acquisition protocols.
**Developed production-grade unit and integration tests** for ML pipelines and models while supporting frontend integration to ensure reliability, maintainability, and seamless product deployment.
Utilized LLM-based NLP pipelines to extract structured medical imaging findings from radiology reports for training medical imaging models.
**Balanced full-time PhD research alongside production ML development**, consistently delivering high-impact technical contributions across research, infrastructure, and deployment initiatives
metrics: Multi-modal registration models across CT, MRI, and X-ray imaging
Abnormality detection models
Production-ready 2D/3D segmentation models across multiple modalities
Imaging workflow automation via MRI sequence classification & plane detection
Asynchronous distributed ML orchestration via Google Pub/Sub
Production unit/integration testing for ML reliability and deployment stability

bullets: Architected an in-house X-ray image registration pipeline that **outperformed a proposed vendor solution**, establishing the foundation for future multi-modal registration systems across CT, MRI, spine, lung, and mammography imaging.

Identified technical and business limitations in third-party imaging solutions through independent benchmarking and experimentation, driving strategic internal AI capability development, **reducing cost and dependency on external vendors**.
Collaborated directly with the Chief Innovation Officer to rapidly **prototype high-impact medical imaging AI concepts** and turned into product roadmaps.
Trained and developed medical image segmentation and classification models.

Expanded beyond core modeling responsibilities by contributing to data curation, annotation strategy, experimentation pipelines, and codebase development, enabling faster iteration across multiple imaging AI projects.
metrics: Replaced external vendor registration solution → avoided procurement/licensing costs
Seeded future registration systems across X-ray, CT, MRI, spine, lung, and mammography imaging
Developed multiple AI proof-of-concepts with executive leadership
Accelerated imaging AI experimentation and model iteration workflows
Improved registration quality over vendor benchmark

org: Robotics and Control Laboratory, University of British Columbia & BC Cancer
bullets: **Published 11 peer-reviewed research papers** and presented work at **leading medical imaging journals and conferences**, advancing state-of-the-art methods in prostate cancer imaging, elastography, and AI-driven intervention systems.

**Led** end-to-end clinical data acquisition and developed multi-modal analysis pipelines for the **OPTiMAL clinical trial** (BC Cancer & UBC), integrating MRI and ultrasound data from operating rooms and clinical environments for quantitative prostate cancer research.

Developed **three ultrasound elastography systems (3D + multi-frequency shear wave imaging)** by solving deep algorithmic and hardware bottlenecks, **unblocking projects stalled for 10+ years** and delivering publications in IEEE TMI, IEEE TUFFC, and IUS.

Designed and implemented **novel adversarial learning-based AI systems for automated prostate brachytherapy planning**, reducing plan generation time from **~20 minutes to ~3 seconds (~400× speedup)** (MICCAI, IPCAI).

Developed registration pipelines for transperineal template mapping biopsy, integrating multi-modal imaging (ultrasound, MRI) and multi-parametric data (anatomical, functional) to enable accurate targeting and treatment delivery.

**Extended capabilities of commercial micro-ultrasound systems (ExactVu)** by enabling shear wave and strain imaging on high-frequency probes, validating new clinical use cases for next-generation prostate imaging platforms.
Operated at the intersection of AI, imaging, and hardware systems by designing, debugging, and maintaining custom medical devices critical to continuing clinical data collection.
Collaborated with oncologists, radiologists, and imaging technologists in OR and clinical environments, ensuring robust and reproducible data acquisition under real-world constraints.
**Mentored PhD/MSc students and interns**, contributing to project execution, research quality, and hiring decisions within a multidisciplinary team.

**Contributed to Liver Fibrosis study, Radical Prostatectomy study, GAN-based training for DaVinci tool tracking in robotic surgery, implementation of 3D imaging in 2D ExactVu microUS systems, and many more.**
metrics: 11 publications in top-tier venues (MICCAI, IEEE TMI, IEEE TUFFC, IEEE IUS, IPCAI)
3 novel elastography systems (3D + multi-frequency SWAVE)
~400× speedup (20 min → ~3 sec planning time)
OPTiMAL clinical trial (multi-modal MRI + ultrasound dataset)
First SWAVE/strain imaging on a commercial ExactVu micro-US
Revived projects stalled for 10 years

org: Electrical and Computer Engineering, University of British Columbia
role: Teaching Assistant (EECE 570: Fundamentals of Visual Computing, ELEC 421: Digital Signal and Image Processing, and Capstone Design Project)
bullets: **Capstone Project (4 academic terms): Supervisory TA of 9 multidisciplinary capstone teams (44 students)** tackling real-world industrial problems, mentoring project execution and unblocking technical challenges, resulting in successful end-to-end solution deliveries.

**EECE 570 (2 academic terms):** Architected and delivered course assignments for this graduate-level course, translating advanced concepts (computer vision, graphics) into applied projects, enhancing student engagement and course quality.
**ELEC 421:** Developed and took lab sessions, bridging theory with hands-on implementation (MATLAB-based workflows), improving practical understanding.
metrics: Supervised/Mentored 44 final year capstone students across 9 teams
Instructed full lab sessions
Designed Graduate and Undergraduate Lab assignments
Mentored final course projects

org: School of Biomedical Engineering, University of British Columbia
bullets: **Deployed end-to-end biomedical instrumentation labs** by designing hands-on experiments that uses clinical devices (ultrasound, ECG, EMG, EEG, oximeters), enabling scalable, real-world training adopted across multiple academic terms.

**Architected and operationalized lab infrastructure** by sourcing, procuring, and configuring diverse medical hardware and simulation systems (tissue phantoms, laparoscopy workstations), ensuring reliability and reproducibility of lab experiments.
**Built long-term academic infrastructure** by designing lab modules still actively used in the curriculum.
**Led onboarding and training** of multiple teaching assistants for conducting lab sessions.
metrics: Designed and deployed the full biomedical instrumentation lab curriculum
Hands-on experiments with clinical devices (ultrasound, ECG, EMG, EEG, oximeters)
Training and Onboarding of multiple TA cohorts
Lab still being used in the curriculum

org: Diagnostic Image Analysis Group (DIAG), Radboud University Medical Center
bullets: Researched if prior/time-series CT scans can improve pulmonary nodule malignancy detection.
**Designed a novel 2.5D CNN multi-timepoint architecture**, improving temporal feature learning while reducing computational complexity.
**Achieved 98% AUC**, a significant jump from the baseline single scan methods.
Conducted extensive ablation studies to systematically evaluate architectural components (e.g., temporal inputs, parallel pathways), driving data-efficient model design decisions.
metrics: Publication in top-tier journal (Radiology)
Proved value of utilizing prior imaging data for lung nodule assessment
98% AUC achieved on a hold out test set
Reduced model complexity vs 3D approaches
Robust performance even on highly imbalanced dataset

org: Leiden University Medical Center & Delft University of Technology (TU Delft)
bullets: **Researched and developed a CNN-based regression model** to automatically estimate the location/orientation of cervical brachytherapy applicator from MRI scans. This enables the possibility of real-time treatment adjustment and therefore better dose optimization.

**Engineered a fully synthetic data generation pipeline** to simulate MRI-like training data under varying anatomical and imaging conditions, enabling model development and unblocking limited data availability in the lab.
**Demonstrated feasibility of end-to-end deep learning** for treatment parameter estimation, introducing a scalable alternative to traditional clinician-driven workflows.
metrics: Proposed 4 different methods of achieving the task
Best method achieved 96% dice score with MSE of 0.4 pixel
Developed synthetic data pipeline enabling model development without real patient data
